NCPPB No. 416: Catalogue name: Xanthomonas arboricola pv. pruni  (Smith 1903) Vauterin et al. 1995
 
Name as received: Xanthomonas pruni
Other name: Xanthomonas campestris pv. pruni (Smith 1903) Dye 1978
Country: New Zealand
Host: Prunus sp. cv. Victory
Year Added: 1957
Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: Yes
Notes: Our records show that when we received this strain from Dye it was listed as "from Victory plum fruit" - as we can find no reference to P. salicina cv. 'Victory' we think this may be from P. domestica cv. 'Victory' (NCPPB 2024).
Other references: This isolate is also in the collections; ATCC 19316, ICMP 51, LMG 852, CFBP 2535
This isolate was isolated by D.W. Dye Tl

NCPPB No. 1062: Catalogue name: Xanthomonas sp.  Dowson 1939

Other name: Xanthomonas campestris (Pammel 1895) Dowson 1939 emend. Vauterin et al. 1995
Country: Papua New Guinea
Host: Dioscorea esculenta
Year Added: 1961
Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: No

Country: USA
Host: Fragaria chiloensis var. ananassa

Pathotype strain: No

NCPPB No. 1826: Catalogue name: Xanthomonas sp.  Dowson 1939
 
Other name: Xanthomonas campestris (Pammel 1895) Dowson 1939 emend. Vauterin et al. 1995
Country: UK
Host: Prunus domestica
Year Added: 1966
Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: No
Notes: Partial gene sequencing of gyrB (Parkinson et al. 2009) places this strain in Xanthomonas arboricola. Not virulent on plum fruitlets (Jones pers. comm.). Pathovar unspecified. Fatty acid profile typical of Xanthomonas but close to X. citri pv. glycines
Other references: This isolate was isolated by G.E. Jones C555

NCPPB No. 2856: Catalogue name: Xanthomonas sp.  Dowson 1939
 
Other name: Xanthomonas campestris (Pammel 1895) Dowson 1939 emend. Vauterin et al. 1995
Country: Netherlands
Host: Chrysanthemum morifolium
Year Added: 1975
Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: No
Notes: Partial gene sequencing of gyrB (Parkinson et al. 2009) places this strain in Xanthomonas arboricola. Pathovar unspecified. Not pathogenic on Chrysanthemum morifolium (NCPPB). Fatty acid profile typical of Xanthomonas. Close to X.arboricola pv. pruni and pv.corylina
Other references: This isolate was isolated by F.W. Catton C798
NCPPB No. 2857: Catalogue name: Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris  (Pammel 1895) Dowson 1939

Host: Brassica napus

Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: No
Other references: This isolate was isolated by W.F. Catton C799
NCPPB No. 2859: Catalogue name: Stenotrophomonas sp.   
 
Name as received: Xanthomonas corylina
Other name: Xanthomonas campestris pv. corylina (Miller et al. 1940) Dye 1978
Country: Turkey
Host: Corylus maxima
Year Added: 1975
Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: No
Notes: Not a Xanthomonas. 16s rRNA sequncing, gyrB sequencing, MALDI-TOF MS, Fatty acid profiling, all suggest Stenotrophomonas sp. PCR and Microsats analysis also suggest not a Xanthomonas sp.
Other references: The source of this isolate was ex D. Massfeller ex R. Hazneci

Host: Azadirachta indica
Year Added: 1977
Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: No

NCPPB No. 2930: Catalogue name: Xanthomonas vasicola pv. holcicola  (Elliott 1930) Vauterin et al. 1995
 
Name as received: Xanthomonas holcicola Elliott 1930
Other name: Xanthomonas campestris pv. holcicola (Elliott 1930) Dye 1978
Country: Solomon Isles
Host: Sorghum vulgare
Year Added: 1977
Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: No
Notes: Does not react with Xvh specific PCR primers - Fatty Acid Profiling indicates this is probably a Sphingomonas sp.
Other references: The donor reference is B5806
This isolate was isolated by ex G. Jackson PP223

Pathotype strain: No
Other references: This isolate was isolated by J.F. Bradbury B5830

Host: Datura metel

Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: Yes

NCPPB No. 2933: Catalogue name: Xanthomonas sp.  Dowson 1939
 
Other name: Xanthomonas campestris (Pammel 1895) Dowson 1939 emend. Vauterin et al. 1995
Country: UK
Host: Calendula officinalis
Year Added: 1977
Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: No
Notes: Partial gene sequencing of gyrB (Parkinson et al. 2009) places this strain in Xanthomonas hortorum. Pathovar unspecified. Fatty acid profile typical of Xanthomonas. Close to X. hortorum pv. hederae
Other references: The donor reference is B6132
This isolate was isolated by J.F. Bradbury B6132
NCPPB No. 2934: Catalogue name: Xanthomonas sp.  Dowson 1939
 
Other name: Xanthomonas campestris (Pammel 1895) Dowson 1939 emend. Vauterin et al. 1995
Country: India
Host: Cayratia carnosa
Year Added: 1977
Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: No
Notes: Pathovar unspecified.Fatty acid profile typical of xanthomonas.Close to X. translucens
Other references: The donor reference is B6141
This isolate was isolated by ex P.S. Shekhawat XWl

NCPPB No. 2982: Catalogue name: Xanthomonas cissicola  (Takimoto 1939) Rudra and Gupta 2021

Other name: Xanthomonas sp.

Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: No

This isolate was isolated by ex M. Goto PC1

Notes: Type C.(=X.c.aurantifolia). Partial gene (gyrB) sequence (NCPPB Aug 2017) indicates this is likely to be Xanthomonas citri pv. aurantifolii
Other references: The donor reference is ISBF 380

NCPPB No. 4356: Catalogue name: Xanthomonas hortorum pv. cynarae  Morinière et al. (2020)
 
Name as received: Xanthomonas cynarae
Country: France - Brittainy
Host: Cynara scolymus (artichoke)
Year Added: 2006
Type strain of the species: No
Pathotype strain: Yes
Notes: Causes water soaked spots on bracts then dark green spots on abaxial parts of capitulum bracts. Large brown necrotic spots result. Gram -Ve, non sporing straight rods, single polar flagellum. 2 days on YBGA at 28 celsius = 2mm colonies.
Other references: The donor reference is CFBP 4188
This isolate is also in the collections; CFBP 4188, ICMP 16775
The source of this isolate was CFBP
Sequencing Link: GenBank: EU498846 - dnaK EU498946 - fyuA EU499061 - gyrB EU499181 - rpoD PRJDB10667 JF409702 - rpoB MT760328 - 16S ribosomal RNA
